columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: The date and time when the user clicked on the link

columns.name: Url
columns.description: The full URL that was clicked on by the user

columns.name: ActionType
columns.description: Indicates whether the click was allowed or blocked by Safe Links or blocked due to a tenant policy, for instance, from Tenant Allow Block list

columns.name: AccountUpn
columns.description: User Principal Name of the account that clicked on the link

columns.name: Workload
columns.description: The application from which the user clicked on the link, with the values being Email, Office, and Teams

columns.name: NetworkMessageId
columns.description: The unique identifier for the email that contains the clicked link, generated by Microsoft 365

columns.name: ThreatTypes
columns.description: Verdict at the time of click, which tells whether the URL led to malware, phish or other threats

columns.name: DetectionMethods
columns.description: Detection technology that was used to identify the threat at the time of click

columns.name: IPAddress
columns.description: Public IP address of the device from which the user clicked on the link

columns.name: IsClickedThrough
columns.description: Indicates whether the user was able to click through to the original URL (1) or not (0) on the Safe Links warning page using "Continue anyway" option

columns.name: UrlChain
columns.description: For scenarios involving redirections, it includes URLs present in the redirection chain

columns.name: ReportId
columns.description: The unique identifier for a click event. For clickthrough scenarios, report ID would have same value, and therefore it should be used to correlate a click event

columns.name: AppName
columns.description: The application's display name as exposed by the associated service principal

columns.name: AppVersion
columns.description: Version of the client application where the click occurred

columns.name: SourceId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the source of the click

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: The type of agent the event was collected by. For example, `OpsManager` for Windows agent, either direct connect or Operations Manager, `Linux` for all Linux agents, or `Azure` for Azure Diagnostics

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The date and time when the user clicked on the link. The value is identical to `Timestamp` and intended for Microsoft Defender for Endpoints queries compatibility
